dockerSettings.title: Dockerfile Detected
dockerSettings.subtitle: Your project will be built and deployed using its Dockerfile
dockerSettings.infoCard: No build settings needed - your Dockerfile defines the build process. Just configure the port your application listens on.
dockerSettings.portHint: The port your container exposes (from EXPOSE or CMD)

buildSettings.sourceFolderDesc: Build from a subdirectory inside the repository or local project.
buildSettings.buildImageDesc: Builder container image for cloud or server builds. Override it when the detected base image is wrong.
buildSettings.productionPathsDesc: Only deploy these files/dirs after build - hides source code from runtime. Leave empty to run in-place.

projectSettings.selectHint: Select your framework to auto-configure build settings

routingSection.title: Routing
routingSection.descDetected: Rewrites, redirects & headers — served on one domain.

environmentVariables.title: Environment Variables
environmentVariables.emptyHintEditing: Click "Add Variable", use "Paste .env", upload a file, or paste anywhere inside this box
environmentVariables.pasteHint: Paste a full .env block anywhere inside this box.

toast.title: Environment Variables
toast.portSet: Runtime port set to {port} from PORT
toast.clipboardUnavailable: Clipboard access is not available here. Click inside the environment box and paste with Cmd/Ctrl+V.
toast.clipboardInvalid: Clipboard does not contain valid KEY=VALUE environment variables
toast.clipboardBlocked: Clipboard access was blocked. Click inside the environment box and paste with Cmd/Ctrl+V.

domain.duplicateDescription: Two routes point at the same domain — change one so each route has its own:

shared.title: Shared environment
shared.optionalVars: Optional vars applied to every service
shared.modalSubtitle: Applied to every service. Service variables win on conflict.

config.title: Configuration
config.cloudPortIgnored: On cloud the published port is ignored — only the container port is routed.
config.statefulWarnPart1: Stateful service on cloud: data lives on the workspace's persistent disk (survives restarts) but is
config.statefulWarnPart2: unless backups are enabled. Docker volume mounts don't apply.
config.volumeHint: Named volume or host path → container path.
config.volumeCloudNote: On cloud these mounts don't apply — data persists on the workspace's disk (not across rebuilds without backups).

main.infoPart1: Internal services can reach each other by service name. Enable
main.infoPart2: only for services that should receive internet traffic.
main.singleAppNote: Parsed compose services are kept for later, but this deployment will use the normal single-app build, start command, environment, and domain settings.
main.modeServicesDesc: Deploy every compose service with its own runtime and domain.
main.modeSingleDesc: Use the normal build and start command flow for one app.
main.deploymentModeDesc: {mode} · Switch between service stack and single app handling.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.subtitle: Runs once at the repo root before each app builds — install, codegen, schema sync, etc.

header.title: Monorepo - {count} apps detected
header.singleDesc: Deploying as a single app - one container, one set of commands, all ports exposed.
header.multiDesc: {selected} of {total} selected. Each app deploys to its own port and domain, sharing one workspace install at the repo root.

monorepo.singleNote: Sub-app definitions are kept for later. This deployment uses the normal single-app build and start commands below - every enabled sub-app's port is exposed from the same runtime.

modes.servicesDesc: Deploy every sub-app with its own runtime, port, and domain.
modes.singleDesc: Promote one sub-app's build + start command, expose every app's port from one runtime.

monorepo.deploymentModeDesc: {mode} · Switch between per-app runtimes and single-app handling.

portAdvisory.title: Is that the right port?
portAdvisory.note: Routing points at this port — if it's wrong, requests will fail with a 502.
portAdvisory.body: Nothing responded on port {port}. If your app listens on a different port, set the correct one here.
portAdvisory.bodyService: Service "{service}": nothing responded on port {port}. If it listens on a different port, set the correct one here.
portAdvisory.changeError: Couldn't update the port. Please try again.

deploymentProcessing.warningTitle: Deployment finished, but some services still need attention.

composeDeployment.decisionBannerDefaultMsg: This deployment finished with failed services. Keep it and fix them later, or reject it to restore the previous version.
composeDeployment.preparingContext: Cloning source and preparing the build context...

partial.title: Deployment finished with failed services
partial.body: {failed} of {total} services failed, but the rest of the stack was deployed successfully. You can keep this deployment and fix the failed services later, or reject it and restore the previous deployment.
partial.rejectNote: Rejecting tears down this partial deployment's containers and, if a previous deployment exists, restores it. The deployment record and its build logs are kept in your history so you can review what failed.
